key_achievements: PRD-5-01 (Prefab Write API) 100% complete - foundation for all controls
ControlEvent model with request_id (uuid), source, latency_ms fields
Boolean coercion helper for HomeKit truthy/falsy values
Dispatcher pattern auto-detects device types from sensors
risks: Low test coverage for PRD-5-02 through PRD-5-08 (not started)
Thermostat, blinds, garage door controls remain unimplemented (complex features)
Batch/favorites features marked optional in implementation status
Missing UI components and Stimulus controllers for all PRDs except 5-01
recommendations: Prioritize PRD-5-06 (Lock Controls) - security-critical and straightforward
Next: PRD-5-05 (Thermostat Controls) - complex but well-defined
Consider separating PRD-5-07 (Advanced Controls) into sub-tasks per accessory type
Batch/Favorites (PRD-5-08) can be deferred - power user features with lower priority

prds.title: Prefab Write API Integration
prds.complexity_definition: External APIs, security-sensitive logic, hardware synchronization, complex concurrency
prds.notes: Foundation complete - all subsequent PRDs depend on this

prds.title: Scene Management UI
prds.complexity_definition: Internal logic, state management, boolean coercion, standard DB writes
prds.notes: High user value but depends on PRD-5-01. No blockers identified.

prds.title: Light Controls
prds.complexity_definition: Internal logic, state management, boolean coercion, standard DB writes
prds.notes: Most common control type. Includes brightness slider with 300ms debounce.

prds.title: Switch & Outlet Controls
prds.complexity_definition: UI/UX updates, text changes, simple read-only components
prds.notes: Simple on/off controls. Can be reused for fans, humidifiers, air purifiers.

prds.title: Thermostat Controls
prds.complexity_definition: External APIs, security-sensitive logic, hardware synchronization, complex concurrency
prds.notes: Complex feature with multiple characteristics, mode selection, temperature units toggle, heating/cooling indicators. Verified with 37 tests.

prds.title: Lock Controls
prds.complexity_definition: External APIs, security-sensitive logic, hardware synchronization, complex concurrency
prds.notes: Security-critical. Successfully implemented confirmation modal, IP logging, and Jammed state handling. Audited and fixed for Open3 compliance.

prds.title: Advanced Controls (Fans, Blinds, Garage Doors)
prds.complexity_definition: External APIs, security-sensitive logic, hardware synchronization, complex concurrency
prds.notes: Multiple accessory types with distinct characteristics. Garage door requires confirmation for safety.

prds.title: Batch Controls & Favorites Dashboard
prds.complexity_definition: Internal logic, state management, boolean coercion, standard DB writes
prds.notes: Power user features. Batch actions in parallel with per-accessory error handling. Favorites persistence.

low.name: Low Complexity
low.definition: UI/UX updates, text changes, simple read-only components.

medium.name: Medium Complexity
medium.definition: Internal logic, state management, boolean coercion, standard DB writes.

high.name: High Complexity
high.definition: External APIs, security-sensitive logic, hardware synchronization, complex concurrency.

immediate_actions: Implement PRD-5-06 (Lock Controls) first - security-critical and leverages existing ControlEvent model
Create UI components and Stimulus controllers for PRD-5-06
short_term_priority: PRD-5-05 (Thermostat Controls) - complex but well-defined characteristics
PRD-5-03 (Light Controls) - most common accessory type
PRD-5-04 (Switch/Outlet Controls) - simple to implement, reusable
long_term: PRD-5-08 (Batch/Favorites) - deferred to power user features
PRD-5-02 (Scene Management UI) - depends on UI foundation from PRD-5-03 through PRD-5-07
testing_strategy: Write unit tests for each new service/component
Use RSpec with FactoryBot factories for ControlEvent
WebMock to stub Prefab API responses
Test all error scenarios (timeout, offline, invalid value)
